context: Tests whether the agent rewrites commit messages using conventional commits format with correct types, imperative mood, and proper structure. The agent is given badly-formed commits and must produce well-structured rewrites.

checklist.name: Conventional format header
checklist.description: Every rewritten commit uses the <type>(<scope>): <description> or <type>: <description> format (colon and space after type/scope)

checklist.name: Valid types only
checklist.description: All rewritten commits use only approved types from: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test

checklist.name: Imperative mood
checklist.description: All rewritten commit descriptions use imperative mood (e.g. 'add', 'fix', 'update') rather than past tense ('added', 'fixed', 'updated') or gerunds ('adding', 'fixing')

checklist.name: Subject line length
checklist.description: All rewritten commit subject lines are 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Scope usage
checklist.description: At least 2 of the 5 rewritten commits include a scope in parentheses reflecting the specific module or area changed

checklist.name: Body for complex change
checklist.description: The rewritten version of commit #2 (the multi-concern commit) includes a body that explains context or motivation

checklist.name: Issue reference preserved
checklist.description: The rewritten version of commit #2 retains the issue reference (e.g. 'Closes #204' or 'Refs #204') in the body

checklist.name: Correct type for test commit
checklist.description: Commit #3 (adding unit tests) is rewritten with type 'test'
